heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 25.8 hours on 09 November 2025 (ranking 9th from our records for the longest storm) and it produced a total of 17.83mm of rain at a maximum intensity of 6.6mm per hour.  The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 07 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 1 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.6.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 0 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (217°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 120.4 mph from 08 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, drier conditions in rainfall (down 1.0 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in rainfall (up 1.0 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 0.1 mm), dew point temperature (down 2.7°C), cloud levels (up 260 m), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 131.9m higher than yesterday, 256.1m lower than the past week, 259.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 492m to a maximum of 831m, averaging 824.8m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1080.9m and 1084.7m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; chilly temperatures are forecasted. Also, dry and less humid conditions and calm and still conditions are expected. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times, and light levels will be low during the day. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (4.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
